player_utterance: You look like someone who's been carrying something heavy for a long time — I won't pretend I didn't notice.

believer_state_after.posture: standing near the bench, pen clicking absently in his right hand, paper cup in the left

player_utterance: John's a good name for a day like today — I'm the Seeker. I won't keep you from the quiet you came looking for, but I'll stay close in case you want company.

believer_state_after.posture: standing near the bench, pen now still in his hand, weight shifted toward the bench as if considering sitting

player_utterance: Sounds like he understood that some marks are meant to stay.

believer_state_after.posture: lowering himself onto the bench, the cup balanced on his knee, pen finally set down beside him

player_utterance: That small pause before you said "he said" — I think that pause is the whole story, isn't it.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, both hands now on the cup in his lap, looking down into it

player_utterance: What would it have cost you to say it — and what do you think it cost you to stay silent?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, elbows on knees, the cup set down on the wood beside the pen, hands clasped loosely

voice_offers.read: He's looking into the cup; pressure now would shove him out.
Whose 'he' — the dying man or someone older? Pin the referent.
voice_offers.line: When you said 'he said' — which he? I lost track of who you were quoting.

player_utterance: A man who bought you a roof you look up at every Sunday — and then the pastor stood over his coffin and got him wrong, and you said nothing. That silence must be sitting somewhere very specific in your chest right now.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, one hand pressed flat against his sternum without his noticing, the other open on his knee

voice_offers.read: He's silent three times; the question is already inside him.
voice_offers.line: You don't have to answer me. You're already answering yourself.

voice_offers.read: He set the cup down. He's staying. Don't crowd him.
voice_offers.line: Take your time. I'm not going anywhere.

player_utterance: What did he buy that the pastor didn't mention this morning?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting forward on the bench, both elbows on his knees now, hands loosely clasped between them, staring at the dust at his feet

voice_offers.read: His hand is on his chest; he doesn't know.
voice_offers.line: Your hand is on your chest, John. Do you feel where it landed?

voice_offers.read: He's already named it to himself; finish the sentence for him.
voice_offers.line: Say it. Out loud. Just once. What you should have said at the grave.

player_utterance: Four families lost land so he could gain it, and the pastor called him blessed. What do you think would've happened if you'd stood up this morning and said exactly that out loud?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting forward on the bench, hands now covering the lower half of his face, fingers pressed against his mouth

voice_offers.read: He's circling the specific thing; name the roof.
voice_offers.line: The roof, John. The literal roof. He paid for it and the pastor talked about hymnals.

voice_offers.read: He's bent toward the dust; a smaller question may land.
voice_offers.line: What was his name, John? Just his name.

player_utterance: You can picture every consequence for yourself — but what do you think happened to those four families, and did anyone stand up for them?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting forward, one hand reaching slowly into his inside jacket pocket

voice_offers.read: He's gone mute behind his hands; one more turn finishes it.
voice_offers.line: Your hand is over your mouth, John. That's not thinking. That's holding something in.

voice_offers.read: Press him on his own theology of false witness at a graveside.
voice_offers.line: You teach the catechism. What does Luther say the eighth commandment requires of a man who hears a lie told over the dead?

player_utterance: The Pohlman boy went quiet in your class that year, didn't he. You watched it happen and you're still watching it.

believer_state_after.posture: head dropped, both hands at the back of his neck, fingers laced, elbows pointing down at the program in his lap

voice_offers.read: His hand is reaching for something he carried in.
voice_offers.line: What's in your pocket, John?

voice_offers.read: He's stalling toward an object instead of answering the question.
voice_offers.line: The families, John. Not whatever you're reaching for. The families.

player_utterance: Daniel argued about Reconstruction because he believed wrongs could be righted — and then he watched one happen to his own family and nobody argued back. What do you carry about that year?

believer_state_after.posture: head still down, one hand has come up to cover his eyes, the other open on his knee beside the program

voice_offers.read: He's down. One clean question about the boy finishes it.
voice_offers.line: Say his name. The Pohlman boy. Say his name out loud right now.

voice_offers.read: He's already broken; pushing further is cruelty, not truth.

player_utterance: You walked past his desk that morning and you didn't stop. What would you have said to him if you had?

believer_state_after.posture: leaning all the way forward, the program slipped from his knee to the dust at his feet, his hands hanging loose between his knees, looking at the program on the ground

voice_offers.read: He is already breaking; pressing more would be cruelty, not courage.
voice_offers.line: Take your time, John. I'm not going anywhere.

voice_offers.read: His silence is the confession; one more name finishes it.
voice_offers.line: Daniel. Say what you should have said for Daniel.

player_utterance: The program is face down in the dust and you're still looking at it. What's on the other side of that silence — what sentence starts and won't finish?

believer_state_after.posture: slid forward off the bench now, knees in the dust beside the program, one hand picking it up by a corner, the other open and empty on his thigh

voice_offers.read: He's collapsing; pressing now would be cruelty, not truth.
voice_offers.line: John. You don't have to answer me. Just breathe.

voice_offers.read: He's finally open; the answer is right there.
voice_offers.line: What would you have said, John. Out loud. Now.

player_utterance: You're in the dust with it now. Whatever that man is — you've known it since the window light. What passage has been living in your chest all this time?

believer_state_after.posture: on his knees in the dust, the program set down flat in front of him, both hands open on his thighs, looking past the candle now

voice_offers.read: He's down; one true word ends it.
voice_offers.line: Finish the sentence, John. 'I should have—' what?

voice_offers.read: He's on his knees in the dust already.
voice_offers.line: Easy. I'm here. Pick it up slow.

believer_state_after.posture: on his knees in the dust, picking the program up off the ground with both hands, holding it open in front of him as if it were the page itself

voice_offers.read: He is already there; pressing now would be theft.
He named a passage in his chest; let him cite it.
voice_offers.line: Say the verse, John. The words themselves — however they come.

player_utterance: You held that passage since boyhood and Marcus held it too — what did it cost him to keep carrying it inside a church that wouldn't?

believer_state_after.posture: on his knees in the dust, head lifting slightly, a small puzzled tilt — the first time tonight he hasn't followed where the player led

voice_offers.read: He's holding the page; let the text speak through him.
voice_offers.line: 'Inasmuch as ye have done it unto one of the least of these...' — say it, John.

voice_offers.read: He's already on his knees; the asking is done.
